1: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
2: As he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?
3: Or is it is it gain to him to him , that thou makest thy ways perfect?
4: Will he enter with thee into judgment?
6: And stripped the naked of their clothing.
7: And thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.
8: And the honourable man dwelt in it.
9: And the arms of the fatherless have been broken.
11: And abundance of waters cover thee.
12: And behold the height of the stars, how high they are!
13: Can he judge through the dark cloud?
14: And he walketh in the circuit of heaven.
16: Whose foundation was overflown with a flood:
17: And what can the Almighty do for them?
18: But the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
19: And the innocent laugh them to scorn.
20: But the remnant of them the fire consumeth.
21: Thereby good shall come unto thee.
22: And lay up his words in thine heart.
23: Thou shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacles.
24: And the gold gold of Ophir as the stones of the brooks.
25: And thou shalt have plenty of silver.
26: And shalt lift up thy face unto God.
27: And thou shalt pay thy vows.
28: And the light shall shine upon thy ways.
29: And he shall save the humble person.
30: And it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.
